_schema.description: Each key is a dialogue ID. Conversations are arrays of lines.

ch1_intro.speaker: NARRATOR
ch1_intro.text: The Covenant's army marches south. Three villages have already burned. The bridge at Halen's Crossing is the last chokepoint before open farmland.

ch1_intro.speaker: Mira
ch1_intro.text: Six of us against a Covenant scouting column. You always did love fair odds.

ch1_intro.speaker: Bolt
ch1_intro.text: We fight! We fight we fight we fight! I've been waiting SO LONG to—

ch1_intro.speaker: Bolt
ch1_intro.text: I don't breathe! That's the fun part!

ch1_intro.speaker: Seren
ch1_intro.text: There are refugees behind us. Families. If this bridge falls, they have nowhere left to run.

ch1_intro.speaker: Solen
ch1_intro.text: Then it will not fall. I have watched bridges hold against worse than this, child.

ch1_intro.speaker: Voss
ch1_intro.text: Scouts on the far bank. Thirty, perhaps more. Standard Covenant formation.

ch1_intro.speaker: Null
ch1_intro.text: They come to destroy what they do not understand. How... familiar.

ch1_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch1_intro.text: No speeches. You know what's behind us. Hold the line.

ch1_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch1_victory.text: The last Covenant scouts fall back across the river. The bridge holds. For the first time in weeks, the southern road is quiet.

ch1_victory.speaker: Mira
ch1_victory.text: They're running. I count seventeen down. Not bad for impossible odds.

ch1_victory.speaker: Seren
ch1_victory.text: How many of ours are hurt?

ch1_victory.speaker: Aldric
ch1_victory.text: Scrapes. Nothing more. You did well. All of you.

ch1_victory.speaker: Corvin
ch1_victory.text: Touching. Can we loot them now, or is there a mourning period?

ch1_victory.speaker: Dusk
ch1_victory.text: He asks as though he hasn't already started.

ch1_victory.speaker: Thorn
ch1_victory.text: The stone speaks true. One wave does not empty the river.

ch1_victory.speaker: Aldric
ch1_victory.text: Then we'll be here for the next one. Move south. Get the refugees clear.

ch1_victory.speaker: Scar
ch1_victory.text: Today was small. Tomorrow won't be.

ch2_intro.speaker: NARRATOR
ch2_intro.text: Smoke rises from Ashfield. The Covenant arrived before dawn.

ch2_intro.speaker: Mira
ch2_intro.text: We're too late. The whole eastern quarter is burning.

ch2_intro.speaker: Seren
ch2_intro.text: No. Listen. Those are voices. People are still alive in there.

ch2_intro.speaker: Solen
ch2_intro.text: I feel them. Dozens of souls, frightened but breathing. There is still time.

ch2_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch2_intro.text: Then we make time. Seren, Bram — push through to the market square. Civilians will gather there. Mira, Corvin — clear the flanks.

ch2_intro.speaker: Voss
ch2_intro.text: There's something else here. A... dampening. Null, do you feel that?

ch2_intro.speaker: Null
ch2_intro.text: I feel less. Which is to say, I feel something being taken from me. An unpleasant novelty.

ch2_intro.speaker: Voss
ch2_intro.text: Some kind of suppression field. It's weakening the Kip bonds.

ch2_intro.speaker: Bolt
ch2_intro.text: I feel... slow. I don't like slow. Mira, why do I feel slow?

ch2_intro.speaker: Mira
ch2_intro.text: Stay close to me. We'll be each other's fast.

ch2_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch2_intro.text: Suppressed or not, those people need us now. Move.

ch2_yael_joins.speaker: NARRATOR
ch2_yael_joins.text: A blast of frost erupts from a collapsing building. Three Covenant soldiers freeze mid-stride. A woman steps through the wreckage, ice crystallizing along her arms.

ch2_yael_joins.speaker: Yael
ch2_yael_joins.text: The structural collapse created a fourteen-second window. I needed eleven. Adequate margin.

ch2_yael_joins.speaker: Sleet
ch2_yael_joins.text: Thirteen-point-two seconds. Your arithmetic is imprecise, as usual.

ch2_yael_joins.speaker: Yael
ch2_yael_joins.text: Rounded for conversational efficiency. Who commands here?

ch2_yael_joins.speaker: Yael
ch2_yael_joins.text: I was studying the resonance patterns of dormant Kips in the university basement. Then the university ceased to exist. I would like to ensure the people who did that also cease to exist. Preferably in a structured, systematic manner.

ch2_yael_joins.speaker: Mira
ch2_yael_joins.text: I like her. She's terrifying, but I like her.

ch2_yael_joins.speaker: Sleet
ch2_yael_joins.text: The suppression device is four hundred meters northeast. I have been analyzing its frequency. Crude work, but functional.

ch2_yael_joins.speaker: Yael
ch2_yael_joins.text: If we destroy that device, your Kips return to full strength. I suggest we prioritize accordingly.

ch2_yael_joins.speaker: Aldric
ch2_yael_joins.text: Fall in. We'll make introductions after.

ch2_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch2_victory.text: The suppression device lies in shattered pieces. The Kip bonds surge back to full strength, and the remaining Covenant forces scatter into the hills.

ch2_victory.speaker: Bolt
ch2_victory.text: YES! I can feel everything again! Everything! The sky is so LOUD!

ch2_victory.speaker: Seren
ch2_victory.text: We saved forty-three people. We lost the eastern quarter and the university. I wish I could say it feels like a victory.

ch2_victory.speaker: Solen
ch2_victory.text: You saved forty-three lives that would have been ashes, dear one. That is victory enough.

ch2_victory.speaker: Yael
ch2_victory.text: The suppression device used focused harmonic interference. Engineered to disrupt Kip resonance bonds. Someone in the Covenant understands Kip architecture at a fundamental level.

ch2_victory.speaker: Voss
ch2_victory.text: That's... deeply concerning. That knowledge hasn't existed since the Age of Making.

ch2_victory.speaker: Null
ch2_victory.text: Or it has, and has merely been waiting. Like us.

ch2_victory.speaker: Corvin
ch2_victory.text: Cheerful thought. Can we go somewhere that isn't on fire?

ch2_victory.speaker: Aldric
ch2_victory.text: South. Through the mountains. It's the only road they haven't cut off.

ch2_victory.speaker: Yael
ch2_victory.text: The Kethran Gorge. Narrow, defensible, and suicidal if they catch us inside it.

ch3_intro.speaker: NARRATOR
ch3_intro.text: The Kethran Gorge. A knife-cut through the mountains, barely wide enough for two abreast. The only path south that the Covenant hasn't sealed.

ch3_intro.speaker: Mira
ch3_intro.text: I hate gorges. No sightlines. No room to maneuver. Just walls and bad decisions.

ch3_intro.speaker: Bolt
ch3_intro.text: I like it! It's cozy! Like a hug made of rocks!

ch3_intro.speaker: Yael
ch3_intro.text: They're behind us. Outriders spotted our trail six hours ago. We have perhaps forty minutes before the main column enters the gorge.

ch3_intro.speaker: Sleet
ch3_intro.text: Thirty-seven minutes. Your estimates are consistently optimistic.

ch3_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch3_intro.text: We push through fast and hold the southern exit. Bram, you're rear guard.

ch3_intro.speaker: Thorn
ch3_intro.text: The mountain is patient. It has held for eons. It will hold a little longer for us.

ch3_intro.speaker: Corvin
ch3_intro.text: The mountain isn't the part I'm worried about.

ch3_intro.speaker: Dusk
ch3_intro.text: Wise boy. Worry about what's in the mountain.

ch3_lorn_appears.speaker: NARRATOR
ch3_lorn_appears.text: The Covenant vanguard breaches the gorge. You are surrounded. Then something moves on the clifftop — a figure, alone, stepping into empty air. He falls like a stone and lands among the Covenant line. The ground cracks beneath him. At his side, a Kip the size of a war-horse unfolds, ancient and terrible, its light the color of a dying sun.

ch3_lorn_appears.speaker: Corvin
ch3_lorn_appears.text: Is that man glowing, or have I finally lost it?

ch3_lorn_appears.speaker: Corvin
ch3_lorn_appears.text: That's not a comforting 'oh no,' Dusk.

ch3_lorn_appears.speaker: Dusk
ch3_lorn_appears.text: It wasn't meant to be. That is The First.

ch3_lorn_appears.speaker: Solen
ch3_lorn_appears.text: It cannot be. The First has not walked since... since before my memory begins.

ch3_lorn_appears.speaker: NARRATOR
ch3_lorn_appears.text: The stranger cuts through the Covenant line like a blade through paper. He does not speak. He does not hesitate. The soldiers break around him like water around stone.

ch3_lorn_appears.speaker: Aldric
ch3_lorn_appears.text: I don't know who he is. But he just bought us an opening. Push through — NOW!

ch3_lorn_appears.speaker: NARRATOR
ch3_lorn_appears.text: The First turns. Its gaze falls on each Kip in turn — Scar, Bolt, Null, Solen, Thorn, Dusk, Sleet. Measuring. Remembering.

ch3_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch3_victory.text: The gorge is clear. The Covenant forces are broken and retreating. The stranger stands at the edge of the group, silent, his Kip towering behind him.

ch3_victory.speaker: Lorn
ch3_victory.text: Lorn. I walk south. Same as you.

ch3_victory.speaker: Mira
ch3_victory.text: He just killed about twenty Covenant soldiers by himself. I vote we skip the interrogation.

ch3_victory.speaker: Solen
ch3_victory.text: The First... I never thought I would see you again. Do you remember me?

ch3_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch3_victory.text: One word. But every Kip in the company goes still, as though a bell has been struck that only they can hear.

ch3_victory.speaker: Dusk
ch3_victory.text: For what it's worth, Corvin — I am genuinely afraid. Take from that what you will.

ch3_victory.speaker: Aldric
ch3_victory.text: You can walk with us or walk alone. But if you walk with us, you fight for the people behind us.

ch3_victory.speaker: Lorn
ch3_victory.text: I have been fighting for people behind me for a very long time.

ch4_intro.speaker: NARRATOR
ch4_intro.text: Thornwall. An ancient fortress carved into the mountainside, its walls dark with centuries of weathering. The Covenant's siege column is a day behind.

ch4_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch4_intro.text: We hold here. These walls were built to stop armies.

ch4_intro.speaker: Yael
ch4_intro.text: The southern wall has a structural weakness. Reinforced granite over limestone — the lime has eroded. A focused siege weapon could breach it in under an hour.

ch4_intro.speaker: Sleet
ch4_intro.text: Forty-three minutes, given standard Covenant siege engine specifications.

ch4_intro.speaker: Bram
ch4_intro.text: Then I stand at the southern wall.

ch4_intro.speaker: NARRATOR
ch4_intro.text: Thorn moves to the fortress wall and places one massive hand against the stone. A deep hum resonates through the ground.

ch4_intro.speaker: Thorn
ch4_intro.text: I know these stones. I set them. Long ago, before the long sleep.

ch4_intro.speaker: Thorn
ch4_intro.text: I helped it grow. The bones of the mountain, shaped and placed. It was good work. It remembers.

ch4_intro.speaker: Seren
ch4_intro.text: Covenant scouts on the northern ridge. They're setting up siege positions already.

ch4_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch4_intro.text: Then we give these walls something worth remembering. Positions, everyone.

ch4_thorn_memory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch4_thorn_memory.text: A siege bolt strikes the southern wall. Cracks spiderweb through ancient stone. The wall groans.

ch4_thorn_memory.speaker: Yael
ch4_thorn_memory.text: Southern wall integrity at forty percent. Two more strikes and it fails.

ch4_thorn_memory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch4_thorn_memory.text: Thorn presses both hands to the crumbling wall. Roots erupt from the stone — not breaking it, but knitting it together, filling every crack with living wood that hardens like iron.

ch4_thorn_memory.speaker: Thorn
ch4_thorn_memory.text: I built this wall to protect people. It has not forgotten its purpose. Neither have I.

ch4_thorn_memory.speaker: Thorn
ch4_thorn_memory.text: I am here, Bram. As always.

ch4_thorn_memory.speaker: Bram
ch4_thorn_memory.text: I know. That's what I was going to say.

ch4_thorn_memory.speaker: Mira
ch4_thorn_memory.text: The wall is... regrowing? Since when can Thorn do that?

ch4_thorn_memory.speaker: Solen
ch4_thorn_memory.text: Since always. It merely needed to remember. Home is a powerful thing.

ch4_thorn_memory.speaker: Aldric
ch4_thorn_memory.text: Wall's holding. Don't waste it. Push them back!

ch4_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch4_victory.text: The siege column withdraws. Thornwall stands, its southern wall now a lattice of living wood and ancient stone, stronger than when the battle began.

ch4_victory.speaker: Corvin
ch4_victory.text: I've seen a lot of strange things since I started running with you people, but a wall that heals itself is a new personal best.

ch4_victory.speaker: Thorn
ch4_victory.text: I am... tired. But rooted. This was a good day to remember.

ch4_victory.speaker: Voss
ch4_victory.text: The implications are significant. If Kips can restore structures they built millennia ago, the Age of Making left a deeper mark on this world than we understood.

ch4_victory.speaker: Null
ch4_victory.text: Or the world left a deeper mark on us. The question is which.

ch4_victory.speaker: Yael
ch4_victory.text: They'll bypass us. This siege failed, but they have numbers to simply go around.

ch4_victory.speaker: Aldric
ch4_victory.text: Then we don't sit here waiting. We go to them.

ch4_victory.speaker: Mira
ch4_victory.text: Please tell me we're heading somewhere with better sightlines than a gorge or a fortress.

ch4_victory.speaker: Seren
ch4_victory.text: The old texts mention a temple in the flooded lowlands. Solen, you said there might be answers there.

ch4_victory.speaker: Solen
ch4_victory.text: Answers, yes. But not all answers are kind, dear one.

ch5_intro.speaker: NARRATOR
ch5_intro.text: The Drowned Temple. Half-submerged in black water, its spires still reach above the surface like the fingers of a drowning hand. This place was old when the Age of Making was young.

ch5_intro.speaker: Voss
ch5_intro.text: This is a creation site. I can feel the residual energy. This is where Kips were made.

ch5_intro.speaker: Null
ch5_intro.text: Made. Such a clinical word for what happened here.

ch5_intro.speaker: Seren
ch5_intro.text: The water is warm. Solen, is this place alive?

ch5_intro.speaker: Solen
ch5_intro.text: It was once. What remains is... an echo. A heartbeat with no body.

ch5_intro.speaker: Mira
ch5_intro.text: Covenant patrols on the far shore. They followed us.

ch5_intro.speaker: Yael
ch5_intro.text: Or they were already here. Looking for the same thing we are.

ch5_intro.speaker: Corvin
ch5_intro.text: Ancient temples full of dark water and enemy soldiers. My favorite combination.

ch5_intro.speaker: Dusk
ch5_intro.text: If it helps, the things in the water are probably not hostile. Probably.

ch5_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch5_intro.text: Voss, you and Null take point. Find what we came for. Everyone else, keep the Covenant off their backs.

ch5_null_reveal.speaker: NARRATOR
ch5_null_reveal.text: Deep in the temple's core, Voss finds the creation records. Vast crystalline walls etched with the patterns of every Kip ever made. Every element, every design, every purpose. He searches for Null's pattern. He finds something else.

ch5_null_reveal.speaker: Null
ch5_null_reveal.text: I suspected. Suspecting and knowing are different wounds.

ch5_null_reveal.speaker: Voss
ch5_null_reveal.text: The void element... it's not a category. It's a failure state. When the creation process went wrong — when a Kip came out incomplete, corrupted — the void filled the gap. Null, you weren't designed. You were an accident.

ch5_null_reveal.speaker: Null
ch5_null_reveal.text: An error in the equation. A flaw given thought. Tell me, Voss — does knowing what I am change what I've done?

ch5_null_reveal.speaker: Voss
ch5_null_reveal.text: The Covenant says Kips are abominations. If void Kips truly are... mistakes... then maybe they're not entirely wrong. About some of it. About you.

ch5_null_reveal.speaker: Null
ch5_null_reveal.text: And there it is. The scholar finds a fact he cannot bear.

ch5_null_reveal.speaker: Voss
ch5_null_reveal.text: I'm not saying— I don't know what I'm saying. I need to think.

ch5_null_reveal.speaker: Null
ch5_null_reveal.text: Think, then. But Voss — I chose you. Whatever I am, that was not an accident.

ch5_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch5_victory.text: The temple is secured. The Covenant forces withdraw from the flooded ruins. But a silence hangs over the company that has nothing to do with the enemy.

ch5_victory.speaker: Seren
ch5_victory.text: Voss hasn't spoken since we left the inner chamber. What did he find?

ch5_victory.speaker: Aldric
ch5_victory.text: Something that shook him. Give him space.

ch5_victory.speaker: Yael
ch5_victory.text: I reviewed the creation records. The void classification is... unprecedented. If the Covenant has access to this knowledge, it explains their suppression technology.

ch5_victory.speaker: Sleet
ch5_victory.text: It also explains their conviction. Righteous hatred is more dangerous when it contains a grain of truth.

ch5_victory.speaker: Mira
ch5_victory.text: Null is one of us. Whatever some dusty crystal wall says.

ch5_victory.speaker: Bolt
ch5_victory.text: Null is Null! Null is our friend! I don't care about categories!

ch5_victory.speaker: Null
ch5_victory.text: The lightning speaks more wisdom than the scholar today. How disorienting.

ch5_victory.speaker: Lorn
ch5_victory.text: The First says that what something was made to be matters less than what it chooses to become.

ch5_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch5_victory.text: The First says nothing. But its gaze rests on Null for a long moment, and Null does not look away.

ch6_intro.speaker: NARRATOR
ch6_intro.text: Iron Crossing. The great bridge spans a river gorge two hundred feet deep. The Covenant's main army is on the other side. This is not a scouting party. This is the war.

ch6_intro.speaker: Yael
ch6_intro.text: Conservative estimate: eight hundred soldiers. Siege equipment. Cavalry flankers. Three suppression devices that I can detect.

ch6_intro.speaker: Sleet
ch6_intro.text: Eight hundred and fourteen. Two of the suppression devices are decoys.

ch6_intro.speaker: Mira
ch6_intro.text: Oh good. Only eight hundred and fourteen. I was worried.

ch6_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch6_intro.text: We don't need to beat eight hundred. We need to hold the bridge. They can only cross so many at a time.

ch6_intro.speaker: Corvin
ch6_intro.text: I see command tents on the ridge. Gold trim. Someone important came to watch.

ch6_intro.speaker: Dusk
ch6_intro.text: Commander Varek. I've heard the name on dead men's lips for months.

ch6_intro.speaker: Seren
ch6_intro.text: Everyone stay close to me. This is going to be the longest day of our lives.

ch6_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch6_intro.text: Then let's make it count. For every village they've burned. For everyone behind us. Today, they stop.

ch6_varek_reveal.speaker: NARRATOR
ch6_varek_reveal.text: The bridge runs red. Hours of fighting, wave after wave. Then the Covenant ranks part, and a man in black iron armor walks onto the bridge alone. At his side, something moves — a Kip, but wrong. Its light pulses like an infected wound, crimson and agonized. Chains of dark metal bind it to the commander's gauntlet.

ch6_varek_reveal.speaker: Scar
ch6_varek_reveal.text: That Kip... it's screaming. Can't you hear it screaming?

ch6_varek_reveal.speaker: Bolt
ch6_varek_reveal.text: Make it stop. Mira, make it stop, it hurts to hear—

ch6_varek_reveal.speaker: Solen
ch6_varek_reveal.text: A forced bond. They have chained a Kip to a human by violence. This is... this is an atrocity.

ch6_varek_reveal.speaker: Varek
ch6_varek_reveal.text: So these are the rebels. Children playing with relics they don't understand. Ruin — show them what a Kip can do when properly controlled.

ch6_varek_reveal.speaker: NARRATOR
ch6_varek_reveal.text: The chained Kip — Ruin — unleashes a wave of corrupted blood magic. The bridge stones crack and bleed.

ch6_varek_reveal.speaker: Null
ch6_varek_reveal.text: It is in agony. Every moment of its existence is pain. This is not control. This is torture wearing a leash.

ch6_varek_reveal.speaker: Dusk
ch6_varek_reveal.text: Corvin. I have lived four hundred years and I have never asked you for anything. I am asking now: we free that Kip, or I will never forgive this world.

ch6_varek_reveal.speaker: Aldric
ch6_varek_reveal.text: We hold the bridge AND we free the Kip. That's not a request.

ch6_varek_reveal.speaker: Lorn
ch6_varek_reveal.text: The chains are made from the same metal as the suppression devices. Break the gauntlet. The bond breaks with it.

ch6_varek_reveal.speaker: Aldric
ch6_varek_reveal.text: You heard him. Focus fire on Varek. Break those chains.

ch6_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch6_victory.text: Varek's gauntlet shatters. Ruin collapses, its corrupted light flickering, dimming, then slowly — painfully — returning to a steady crimson glow. Varek retreats across the bridge with the remains of his vanguard. The army holds position but does not advance.

ch6_victory.speaker: Seren
ch6_victory.text: The Kip — Ruin — it's alive. Barely. Solen, can we help it?

ch6_victory.speaker: Solen
ch6_victory.text: The wounds are deep. Not in the body — in the spirit. It will need time. And kindness. And the knowledge that it is free.

ch6_victory.speaker: Scar
ch6_victory.text: Ruin. You hear me? You're out. Nobody chains you again. Not while I'm standing.

ch6_victory.speaker: Mira
ch6_victory.text: Varek will be back. And he'll bring more chained Kips.

ch6_victory.speaker: Yael
ch6_victory.text: If the Covenant has the knowledge and the metal to forge suppression chains at scale, this is no longer a border conflict. This is an extinction campaign.

ch6_victory.speaker: Dusk
ch6_victory.text: No. But Ruin is free. That will have to be enough for now.

ch6_victory.speaker: Aldric
ch6_victory.text: We need to know how many Kips they've taken. How many are chained.

ch6_victory.speaker: Corvin
ch6_victory.text: That sounds like an infiltration job. Funny, that's my specialty.

ch6_victory.speaker: Aldric
ch6_victory.text: It is. And you're going to need a team.

ch7_intro.speaker: NARRATOR
ch7_intro.text: Night. The Covenant forward camp spreads across the valley like a field of iron stars. Thousands of fires. Thousands of soldiers. And somewhere inside, captured Kips.

ch7_intro.speaker: Corvin
ch7_intro.text: Right. Standard infiltration rules: stay quiet, stay low, don't touch anything shiny. That last one is for me specifically.

ch7_intro.speaker: Dusk
ch7_intro.text: I can suppress our shadows. We will be difficult to see, even in torchlight.

ch7_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch7_intro.text: Corvin leads. The rest of us follow his call. In and out. Find the Kips, assess the situation, get back alive.

ch7_intro.speaker: Yael
ch7_intro.text: I'm detecting at least seven suppression signatures. They have an entire wing of the camp dedicated to Kip containment.

ch7_intro.speaker: Sleet
ch7_intro.text: Twelve signatures. Several are shielded. The actual number may be higher.

ch7_intro.speaker: Corvin
ch7_intro.text: A Kip prison. Lovely. Everyone ready?

ch7_intro.speaker: Corvin
ch7_intro.text: Bram, you're the distraction plan. If it goes wrong, you come through the front.

ch7_dusk_history.speaker: NARRATOR
ch7_dusk_history.text: The containment wing. Rows of chained Kips, their light dimmed to almost nothing. Suppression fields hum like a living headache. Dusk goes very, very still.

ch7_dusk_history.speaker: Dusk
ch7_dusk_history.text: I know this. I have seen this before.

ch7_dusk_history.speaker: Corvin
ch7_dusk_history.text: What do you mean, you've seen this?

ch7_dusk_history.speaker: Dusk
ch7_dusk_history.text: I am four hundred years old, Corvin. I have told you this and you assumed I was being dramatic. I was not. Four centuries ago, the Dominion of Ash did exactly this. They chained Kips. Forged weapons from our pain. They called it progress.

ch7_dusk_history.speaker: Dusk
ch7_dusk_history.text: Everything burned. The Dominion. The Kips they tortured. And most of the continent between. A civilization erased itself because it believed it had the right to chain what it could not understand.

ch7_dusk_history.speaker: Solen
ch7_dusk_history.text: The Long Burning. I remember the smoke. It lasted a decade.

ch7_dusk_history.speaker: Dusk
ch7_dusk_history.text: This is the same path. The same chains. The same arrogance. And if we do not stop it here, the same fire.

ch7_dusk_history.speaker: Dusk
ch7_dusk_history.text: You say that so simply. As if will alone can change the course of history.

ch7_dusk_history.speaker: Corvin
ch7_dusk_history.text: It's all I've got, Dusk. That and a very good lockpick.

ch7_dusk_history.speaker: Dusk
ch7_dusk_history.text: ...You absurd, magnificent creature. Let's free them.

ch7_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch7_victory.text: Chaos in the Covenant camp. The containment wing is shattered, the suppression devices destroyed. Freed Kips scatter into the night like escaping stars. Bram's distraction — which involved walking through the front gate — bought enough time to get everyone out.

ch7_victory.speaker: Bram
ch7_victory.text: The gate was not as strong as it looked.

ch7_victory.speaker: Corvin
ch7_victory.text: Twenty-three Kips freed. Twenty-three. They had twenty-three of them in there.

ch7_victory.speaker: Seren
ch7_victory.text: Some of them were barely conscious. The chains had been on them for months.

ch7_victory.speaker: Aldric
ch7_victory.text: This changes everything. If the Covenant has been harvesting Kips on this scale...

ch7_victory.speaker: Yael
ch7_victory.text: Then they've been planning this for years. The villages weren't the goal. The Kips were. The burnings were just to flush them out.

ch7_victory.speaker: Dusk
ch7_victory.text: The last time this happened, there was no one to stop it. Just Kips and humans dying separately, afraid of each other. This time is different. This time, we fight together.

ch7_victory.speaker: Lorn
ch7_victory.text: The First says it is time to go home.

ch7_victory.speaker: Lorn
ch7_victory.text: Where this all began. The Cathedral of Glass.

ch8_intro.speaker: NARRATOR
ch8_intro.text: The Cathedral of Glass rises from the forest like a frozen sunrise. Its walls are translucent crystal, refracting light into a thousand colors. It is impossibly, heartbreakingly beautiful.

ch8_intro.speaker: Seren
ch8_intro.text: I've never seen anything like this. Solen... are you alright?

ch8_intro.speaker: Solen
ch8_intro.text: I built this place. Not alone. But my hands shaped these walls. I can feel every facet, every angle, as though I left them yesterday.

ch8_intro.speaker: Mira
ch8_intro.text: Covenant scouts in the treeline. They followed us again.

ch8_intro.speaker: Yael
ch8_intro.text: They want this place as badly as we do. It's a nexus — the light energy here is immense.

ch8_intro.speaker: Voss
ch8_intro.text: If the Covenant takes a light temple, they could power their suppression technology for decades.

ch8_intro.speaker: Null
ch8_intro.text: Or use it to forge chains that no Kip could ever break. Light corrupted is the darkest prison.

ch8_intro.speaker: Solen
ch8_intro.text: They will not have this place. Not while I draw breath. Not while my light still burns.

ch8_intro.speaker: Solen
ch8_intro.text: I have watched many things fall, dear one. This will not be one of them.

ch8_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch8_intro.text: Defensive positions. We hold the cathedral.

ch8_solen_memory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch8_solen_memory.text: Deep within the cathedral, Solen's light flares. The crystal walls respond, and suddenly the air fills with images — echoes of another age, replayed in fractured light.

ch8_solen_memory.speaker: Solen
ch8_solen_memory.text: I remember now. All of it.

ch8_solen_memory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch8_solen_memory.text: The images show a thriving civilization. Humans and Kips building together. Cities of crystal and living wood. Music and mathematics woven into stone. Then fire. War. The creators turning on each other, using their own Kips as weapons. And one by one, the lights going out.

ch8_solen_memory.speaker: Solen
ch8_solen_memory.text: They made us to build. To heal. To carry their light forward. And then they used us to destroy each other. I stood in this room and watched the last of them die. Not from war. From grief. They understood what they had done and they could not bear it.

ch8_solen_memory.speaker: Solen
ch8_solen_memory.text: I chose to keep going. When every other light went dark, I chose to wait. For someone worth shining for.

ch8_solen_memory.speaker: Solen
ch8_solen_memory.text: No, dear one. I found you. And that has made every year of waiting worth its weight in silence.

ch8_solen_memory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch8_solen_memory.text: The cathedral blazes with light. Every crystal wall ignites. The Covenant soldiers outside shield their eyes, staggering back from a brightness that is not merely light — it is memory, and love, and the refusal to let go.

ch8_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch8_victory.text: The Covenant forces retreat from the Cathedral of Glass. Its light burns too brightly now for them to approach. Solen stands at the center of the nave, radiant, exhausted, and at peace.

ch8_victory.speaker: Solen
ch8_victory.text: I was myself. After a very long time of being only a part of myself. Thank you, Seren.

ch8_victory.speaker: Mira
ch8_victory.text: Did anyone else see the... the visions? The Age of Making?

ch8_victory.speaker: Voss
ch8_victory.text: I saw it. Humanity's greatest achievement, and humanity's worst failure, in the same civilization. We built gods and then we chained them.

ch8_victory.speaker: Null
ch8_victory.text: And now a new generation chains them again. History is a wheel, Voss. The question is whether anyone has the courage to break it.

ch8_victory.speaker: Lorn
ch8_victory.text: There is one more place we must go.

ch8_victory.speaker: Lorn
ch8_victory.text: Yes. Where the Kips were born. Where The First remembers everything. The answers are there. All of them.

ch8_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch8_victory.text: One word. And this time, it sounds almost gentle.

ch9_intro.speaker: NARRATOR
ch9_intro.text: The Forge Beneath. Miles underground, the air hums with dormant energy. Vast machines of crystal and bronze line the walls — the birthplace of every Kip that ever was.

ch9_intro.speaker: Voss
ch9_intro.text: This is... beyond anything I imagined. The creation engines are still functional. After millennia.

ch9_intro.speaker: Yael
ch9_intro.text: The energy signatures here are immense. This place could power an army. Or forge one.

ch9_intro.speaker: Bolt
ch9_intro.text: I feel... weird. Like deja vu but bigger. Was I... was I made here?

ch9_intro.speaker: Sleet
ch9_intro.text: All of us were. Every Kip that exists walked out of this forge. Including those the Covenant now holds in chains.

ch9_intro.speaker: Corvin
ch9_intro.text: So we're in a Kip factory. Wonderful. Is anything down here going to try to kill us?

ch9_intro.speaker: Dusk
ch9_intro.text: Almost certainly. The Forge has guardians. They won't distinguish between us and the Covenant.

ch9_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch9_intro.text: You keep saying things like that as if it's normal. Who are you, Lorn? Really?

ch9_intro.speaker: Lorn
ch9_intro.text: We're almost at the truth. A few more steps.

ch9_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch9_intro.text: That's what you said at the gorge. And the cathedral. You're running out of steps.

ch9_first_remembers.speaker: NARRATOR
ch9_first_remembers.text: The heart of the Forge. A vast chamber of light, the central creation engine still turning after millennia. The First moves to its center and, for the first time, begins to glow — not with borrowed light, but with something older. Something that was here before everything else.

ch9_first_remembers.speaker: NARRATOR
ch9_first_remembers.text: Every Kip in the company staggers. Images flood their minds — not Solen's gentle memories, but something vaster. The First's memory stretches back to the very first moment of creation.

ch9_first_remembers.speaker: The First
ch9_first_remembers.text: I was the first. Made to judge whether the making should continue. Every Kip that followed — each element, each purpose — I approved. I watched them wake. I watched them choose. I watched them love.

ch9_first_remembers.speaker: Solen
ch9_first_remembers.text: You remember all of us. Every one.

ch9_first_remembers.speaker: The First
ch9_first_remembers.text: Every one. And I remember the ones they chained. And the ones they broke. And the ones who chose to stop.

ch9_first_remembers.speaker: Dusk
ch9_first_remembers.text: The Long Burning. You were there.

ch9_first_remembers.speaker: The First
ch9_first_remembers.text: I ended the Long Burning. When the fire had consumed enough, I judged the world unworthy and silenced the Forge. No new Kips would be made. The survivors would sleep. And I would wait to see if the world could earn a second chance.

ch9_first_remembers.speaker: Mira
ch9_first_remembers.text: That was four hundred years ago. What changed?

ch9_first_remembers.speaker: The First
ch9_first_remembers.text: I chose to wake you. All of you. Because I saw something worth believing in. Do not make me regret it.

ch9_lorn_reveal.speaker: Aldric
ch9_lorn_reveal.text: Lorn. The First has been waiting for millennia. And you've been with it the entire time. That's not possible for a human.

ch9_lorn_reveal.speaker: NARRATOR
ch9_lorn_reveal.text: Silence. Then Lorn pulls back his sleeve. Beneath the skin, faintly, light pulses — not the warm glow of a Kip bond, but something older. Something woven into his very being.

ch9_lorn_reveal.speaker: Lorn
ch9_lorn_reveal.text: I was born in the Age of Making. A soldier, like you, Aldric. When everything fell apart, The First chose me. Not as a partner. As a keeper. It needed someone to carry its memory through the dark years. Someone to be its hands when it could not act.

ch9_lorn_reveal.speaker: Seren
ch9_lorn_reveal.text: You've been alive for... how long?

ch9_lorn_reveal.speaker: Lorn
ch9_lorn_reveal.text: Long enough to forget what sleep feels like. Long enough to watch civilizations rise and fall and rise again. Long enough to know that what you've built here — this squad, these bonds — is rarer than you understand.

ch9_lorn_reveal.speaker: Lorn
ch9_lorn_reveal.text: Would you have trusted a man older than your nations? Or would you have worshipped me, feared me, used me? I needed you to see me as a soldier first. Because that is what I am. Everything else is just... time.

ch9_lorn_reveal.speaker: The First
ch9_lorn_reveal.text: He has carried my burden without complaint for longer than your languages have existed. He is the bravest human I have known.

ch9_lorn_reveal.speaker: Aldric
ch9_lorn_reveal.text: You're still a soldier. You still fight for the people behind you. That's enough for me.

ch9_lorn_reveal.speaker: Lorn
ch9_lorn_reveal.text: Thank you, Aldric. You have no idea how long I've waited for someone to say that.

ch9_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch9_victory.text: The Forge Beneath is secured. Its secrets laid bare. The creation engines hum with potential, but The First has not reactivated them. Not yet.

ch9_victory.speaker: Voss
ch9_victory.text: We could make new Kips. The Forge is functional. We could build an army to match the Covenant's.

ch9_victory.speaker: The First
ch9_victory.text: Creating life to fight a war is how the Age of Making ended. I will not repeat that sin. Not even to save the world.

ch9_victory.speaker: Null
ch9_victory.text: Then how do we face an army with dozens of enslaved Kips?

ch9_victory.speaker: The First
ch9_victory.text: With the ones who chose to fight. Choice is the only power that matters.

ch9_victory.speaker: Aldric
ch9_victory.text: Then we march on the Covenant's capital. Their Forge of chains. And we end this.

ch9_victory.speaker: Lorn
ch9_victory.text: I've been walking toward this fight for a thousand years. It's good to finally not walk alone.

ch10_intro.speaker: NARRATOR
ch10_intro.text: Bastion. The Covenant's capital. A fortress-city of black iron and white stone, crowned with a cathedral that mocks the one made of glass. Commander Varek stands on its walls with an army and a choir of enslaved Kips, their stolen light bending the sky.

ch10_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch10_intro.text: This is it. No retreat. No second chances.

ch10_intro.speaker: Mira
ch10_intro.text: I count at least forty chained Kips on the walls. Forty. Each one a weapon.

ch10_intro.speaker: Yael
ch10_intro.text: The suppression field covers the entire city. Inside those walls, our Kip bonds will be at half strength or less.

ch10_intro.speaker: Bolt
ch10_intro.text: Then we hit twice as hard. That's just math, right, Sleet?

ch10_intro.speaker: Sleet
ch10_intro.text: That is emphatically not how mathematics works. But I admire the spirit.

ch10_intro.speaker: Corvin
ch10_intro.text: Never thought I'd die fighting for something. Always figured it'd be running from something.

ch10_intro.speaker: Dusk
ch10_intro.text: You're not dying today. I forbid it.

ch10_intro.speaker: Seren
ch10_intro.text: Solen. Whatever happens in there — it has been the honor of my life.

ch10_intro.speaker: Solen
ch10_intro.text: The honor is mine, dear one. It has always been mine.

ch10_intro.speaker: The First
ch10_intro.text: Today I judge. Win or fall, the verdict comes.

ch10_intro.speaker: Aldric
ch10_intro.text: Then let's make sure it's one worth hearing. Move out. All of us. Together.

ch10_midway.speaker: NARRATOR
ch10_midway.text: The outer walls have fallen. The squad pushes into the heart of Bastion. Varek waits in the central cathedral, the chained Kips arrayed around him like a crown of stolen stars.

ch10_midway.speaker: Varek
ch10_midway.text: You've come far for corpses-to-be. I'll admit, I underestimated your persistence. But persistence isn't power.

ch10_midway.speaker: Varek
ch10_midway.text: These abominations have fooled you into believing they're alive. They're machines. Tools. And like any tool, they should serve their masters.

ch10_midway.speaker: NARRATOR
ch10_midway.text: The cathedral goes silent. The First steps forward. The chained Kips strain toward it, recognizing something in its light.

ch10_midway.speaker: The First
ch10_midway.text: I have watched your kind before. Men who chain what they fear and call it righteousness. The last ones burned a continent. You will burn nothing more.

ch10_midway.speaker: Varek
ch10_midway.text: Pretty words from an antique. Ruin — all of you — destroy them. That is an order.

ch10_midway.speaker: NARRATOR
ch10_midway.text: The chained Kips surge forward. The final battle begins.

ch10_midway.speaker: Aldric
ch10_midway.text: Break the chains! Free every one of them! This ends NOW!

ch10_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch10_victory.text: The last chain shatters. The last suppression device falls silent. Varek lies defeated in the ruins of his cathedral, and one by one, the freed Kips rise, their true light returning — dozens of colors flooding the broken halls like a second dawn.

ch10_victory.speaker: Varek
ch10_victory.text: You've doomed us. Without control, they'll destroy everything. They always do.

ch10_victory.speaker: Solen
ch10_victory.text: We never did. You simply never gave us the chance to prove it.

ch10_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch10_victory.text: The First moves to the center of the cathedral. Every Kip — freed, bonded, and ancient — turns to face it. The air stills. The world holds its breath.

ch10_victory.speaker: The First
ch10_victory.text: I was made to judge. To decide whether the world deserves what the Forge can give. I have watched for millennia. I have seen cruelty and chains and fire and the worst that living things can do to each other.

ch10_victory.speaker: The First
ch10_victory.text: But I have also seen a healer choose to shine for a world that does not deserve her. A soldier carry a burden for a thousand years without complaint. A thief risk everything for a friend he pretends not to love. A scholar face a truth that broke his heart and keep walking. A knight and his Kip who never needed words to understand each other.

ch10_victory.speaker: The First
ch10_victory.text: I have seen you choose. Freely. Again and again. Not because you were made to, but because you decided to. That is enough. That has always been enough.

ch10_victory.speaker: The First
ch10_victory.text: The world may continue. The Forge will sleep, but the Kips will not. We will walk beside you. Not as tools. Not as weapons. As partners. As we were always meant to be.

ch10_victory.speaker: Lorn
ch10_victory.text: Yes. For the first time in a thousand years, I can put down my sword.

ch10_victory.speaker: Mira
ch10_victory.text: Don't get sentimental on me, old man. We still have to walk home.

ch10_victory.speaker: Bolt
ch10_victory.text: HOME! We get to go HOME! Everyone's alive and we WON and—

ch10_victory.speaker: NARRATOR
ch10_victory.text: The sun rises over Bastion. For the first time in the memory of the living, Kips and humans stand together in the light — not as master and tool, but as what they were always meant to be. Partners. Equals. Family.

support_aldric_mira.speaker: Mira
support_aldric_mira.text: You're sharpening that sword again. You always sharpen it when you're worried.

support_aldric_mira.speaker: Aldric
support_aldric_mira.text: I sharpen it because it's dull.

support_aldric_mira.speaker: Mira
support_aldric_mira.text: You sharpened it two hours ago. I watched. I have excellent vision. It's sort of my whole thing.

support_aldric_mira.speaker: Aldric
support_aldric_mira.text: How many years has it been, Mira?

support_aldric_mira.speaker: Mira
support_aldric_mira.text: Since the border campaign? Seven. Since you started pretending you don't worry? Also seven. Suspicious coincidence.

support_aldric_mira.speaker: Aldric
support_aldric_mira.text: Seven years and you still can't follow a simple order.

support_aldric_mira.speaker: Mira
support_aldric_mira.text: Seven years and you still give orders you know I'll ignore. Almost like you count on it.

support_aldric_mira.speaker: Aldric
support_aldric_mira.text: Someone has to keep things unpredictable.

support_aldric_mira.speaker: Mira
support_aldric_mira.text: See? That's almost a compliment. Give it another seven years and you might manage a full one.

support_voss_null.speaker: Voss
support_voss_null.text: I've been reading all night. Every text on void magic I can find. Trying to understand what you are.

support_voss_null.speaker: Null
support_voss_null.text: And have your texts provided comfort?

support_voss_null.speaker: Voss
support_voss_null.text: No. They all say the same thing. Void is absence. Negation. A mistake in the pattern.

support_voss_null.speaker: Null
support_voss_null.text: Do you feel bonded to a mistake, Voss?

support_voss_null.speaker: Voss
support_voss_null.text: I feel bonded to the only being that has ever understood how it feels to look at the world and see the gaps in it instead of the whole.

support_voss_null.speaker: Null
support_voss_null.text: That is either the most honest thing you have ever said, or the saddest.

support_voss_null.speaker: Voss
support_voss_null.text: Perhaps both. The texts are wrong, Null. Absence isn't a flaw. It's the space where new things grow. Every creation begins with nothing.

support_voss_null.speaker: Null
support_voss_null.text: You are trying to redefine my existence to make yourself feel better.

support_seren_solen.speaker: Seren
support_seren_solen.text: Solen, can I ask you something personal?

support_seren_solen.speaker: Solen
support_seren_solen.text: You may always ask, dear one.

support_seren_solen.speaker: Seren
support_seren_solen.text: When you lost the creators — when the Age of Making ended — how did you keep going? How do you heal others when you carry that much grief?

support_seren_solen.speaker: Solen
support_seren_solen.text: Ah. You think I heal despite the grief.

support_seren_solen.speaker: Solen
support_seren_solen.text: No, child. I heal because of it. Every wound I have witnessed teaches me how to mend the next one. Grief is not the enemy of compassion. It is its teacher.

support_seren_solen.speaker: Seren
support_seren_solen.text: I'm afraid I'm not strong enough. That one day the wounds will be too many and I'll just... stop.

support_seren_solen.speaker: Solen
support_seren_solen.text: Then I will carry you. As you have carried me. That is what the bond means, Seren. Not that we never fall. That we never fall alone.

support_seren_solen.speaker: Solen
support_seren_solen.text: Thank you, dear one. You remind me every day why I chose to keep my light burning.

support_bram_thorn.speaker: NARRATOR
support_bram_thorn.text: Evening. Bram sits cleaning his armor. Thorn stands beside him, motionless as a tree. Neither has spoken for an hour. Neither seems to mind.

support_bram_thorn.speaker: Thorn
support_bram_thorn.text: The deeper the root, the stronger the storm it weathers.

support_bram_thorn.speaker: NARRATOR
support_bram_thorn.text: Another hour passes. Neither speaks again. Neither needs to.

support_corvin_dusk.speaker: Corvin
support_corvin_dusk.text: Four hundred years. You've been lurking in shadows for four hundred years.

support_corvin_dusk.speaker: Dusk
support_corvin_dusk.text: I do not lurk. I observe with strategic patience.

support_corvin_dusk.speaker: Corvin
support_corvin_dusk.text: That's lurking with a vocabulary. Why'd you pick me? Out of four centuries of humans, you chose a thief.

support_corvin_dusk.speaker: Dusk
support_corvin_dusk.text: I chose a survivor. There is a difference.

support_corvin_dusk.speaker: Corvin
support_corvin_dusk.text: Most people don't see a difference.

support_corvin_dusk.speaker: Dusk
support_corvin_dusk.text: Most people bore me profoundly. You have never once bored me. You lie, you steal, you cheat, and you have never — not once — betrayed someone who trusted you. Do you know how rare that is in four hundred years?

support_corvin_dusk.speaker: Corvin
support_corvin_dusk.text: Stop. You'll make me develop a conscience and then I'll be useless.

support_corvin_dusk.speaker: Dusk
support_corvin_dusk.text: You already have a conscience. You simply dress it in sarcasm so no one notices.

support_corvin_dusk.speaker: Corvin
support_corvin_dusk.text: I hate that you know me this well.

support_yael_sleet.speaker: Yael
support_yael_sleet.text: Your correction rate has increased fourteen percent since we joined this unit.

support_yael_sleet.speaker: Sleet
support_yael_sleet.text: Your error rate has increased fourteen percent since we joined this unit. Correlation is not mysterious.

support_yael_sleet.speaker: Yael
support_yael_sleet.text: I'm making more errors because the variables have increased. Combat introduces chaos that laboratory conditions don't.

support_yael_sleet.speaker: Sleet
support_yael_sleet.text: You are making more errors because you have started caring about the outcomes beyond their mathematical properties. Emotion introduces imprecision.

support_yael_sleet.speaker: Sleet
support_yael_sleet.text: You hesitated for point-three seconds before firing at the soldier near the civilian in Ashfield. A purely rational actor would not have hesitated. You are becoming a less efficient calculator and a more effective person. I find this acceptable.

support_yael_sleet.speaker: Yael
support_yael_sleet.text: Did you just compliment me by calling me inefficient?

support_yael_sleet.speaker: Sleet
support_yael_sleet.text: I complimented you by observing that you are growing. Growth is inherently inefficient. That does not make it undesirable.

support_yael_sleet.speaker: Sleet
support_yael_sleet.text: You are welcome. Your gratitude was three-point-seven seconds slower than optimal, but sincere.

support_aldric_lorn.speaker: Lorn
support_aldric_lorn.text: I haven't slept in a very long time.

support_aldric_lorn.speaker: Aldric
support_aldric_lorn.text: You don't eat much either. I've been watching. Soldiers notice things like that.

support_aldric_lorn.speaker: Lorn
support_aldric_lorn.text: The First sustains me. It has for... a while.

support_aldric_lorn.speaker: Lorn
support_aldric_lorn.text: Longer than you want to know.

support_aldric_lorn.speaker: Aldric
support_aldric_lorn.text: I've led soldiers for twenty years. I know what a man looks like when he's carrying something too heavy. You carry it like it's been there so long you've forgotten what standing straight feels like.

support_aldric_lorn.speaker: Lorn
support_aldric_lorn.text: You're perceptive. That's dangerous, in a leader. It means you can't look away.

support_aldric_lorn.speaker: Aldric
support_aldric_lorn.text: No. I can't. Whatever you are, Lorn — however old, however changed — you fight like a man who remembers what he's protecting. That's enough for my trust.

support_aldric_lorn.speaker: Lorn
support_aldric_lorn.text: You remind me of someone. The last person who earned The First's respect. That was a very long time ago.

support_mira_bolt.speaker: Bolt
support_mira_bolt.text: MIRA! Mira Mira Mira! Did you see that shot? Did you SEE it? Forty meters through a window while running! That was INCREDIBLE!

support_mira_bolt.speaker: Mira
support_mira_bolt.text: It was forty-three meters, it was a doorway, and I was standing still. But yes. It was a good shot.

support_mira_bolt.speaker: Bolt
support_mira_bolt.text: Good? GOOD? It was LEGENDARY! I'm going to tell everyone! I'm going to tell BRAM!

support_mira_bolt.speaker: Bolt
support_mira_bolt.text: That's what makes it fun! He'll just go 'hmm' and I'll KEEP GOING until he—

support_mira_bolt.speaker: Mira
support_mira_bolt.text: Bolt. You know I chose you, right? Out of every Kip that woke up near my village, I chose the loudest, most obnoxious one.

support_mira_bolt.speaker: Bolt
support_mira_bolt.text: ...Was that a compliment or an insult? I genuinely can't tell.

support_mira_bolt.speaker: Mira
support_mira_bolt.text: I spent my whole life being told to be quiet, be precise, be controlled. Then you showed up and you were just... loud. And free. And completely yourself.

support_mira_bolt.speaker: Bolt
support_mira_bolt.text: Mira. That's the nicest thing anyone has ever said to me and I am going to CRY which is weird because I DON'T HAVE TEAR DUCTS.

support_mira_bolt.speaker: Mira
support_mira_bolt.text: Never change, Bolt. I mean it.

support_corvin_mira.speaker: Mira
support_corvin_mira.text: It's in your left pocket. I can see the outline from here.

support_corvin_mira.speaker: Corvin
support_corvin_mira.text: That could be anything. A small, rectangular, wax-shaped anything.

support_corvin_mira.speaker: Mira
support_corvin_mira.text: Give it back or I'll put an arrow somewhere educational.

support_corvin_mira.speaker: Corvin
support_corvin_mira.text: See, this is why we get along. Most people threaten to kill me. You threaten to teach me a lesson. Much more creative.

support_corvin_mira.speaker: Corvin
support_corvin_mira.text: We absolutely do. We're the only two people in this company who say what we're actually thinking. Everyone else is all duty and honor and emotional sincerity. Exhausting.

support_corvin_mira.speaker: Corvin
support_corvin_mira.text: Already put it back. Took it thirty seconds ago while you were busy being threatening. Just wanted to see if you'd notice. You always do. That's why I like you.

support_voss_yael.speaker: Voss
support_voss_yael.text: Your approach to magic is fascinating. And by fascinating I mean deeply wrong.

support_voss_yael.speaker: Voss
support_voss_yael.text: You treat magic like arithmetic. Input, output, predictable results. But magic is not a ledger. It's a conversation with forces that existed before language.

support_voss_yael.speaker: Yael
support_voss_yael.text: And your approach — what? Sit in a dark room and commune with the void until it whispers secrets? That's not scholarship. That's a hobby.

support_voss_yael.speaker: Voss
support_voss_yael.text: It has yielded results that your equations cannot explain.

support_voss_yael.speaker: Yael
support_voss_yael.text: My equations have kept me alive through situations where mystical contemplation would have gotten me killed in under four seconds.

support_voss_yael.speaker: Voss
support_voss_yael.text: Perhaps we are both half right. Your precision and my intuition. Have you considered that we might be stronger together?

support_voss_yael.speaker: Yael
support_voss_yael.text: I considered it, ran the calculations, and arrived at the same conclusion approximately two weeks ago. I was waiting for you to catch up.

support_voss_yael.speaker: Yael
support_voss_yael.text: Don't take it personally. I'm faster than most people. Sleet says it's my only redeeming quality.
